actions.reasoning: Applies a very aggressive 10th/90th percentile clip that would wash out meaningful credit-risk variation.

actions.reasoning: Rounds the wide numeric statement block to whole numbers, erasing the fine-grained financial variation that the AMEX rounding trick is meant to preserve.

actions.reasoning: Mislabels much more extreme percentile summaries as the benchmark percentile block, so the engineered AMEX distribution features no longer reflect the intended customer-level quantiles.

actions.reasoning: Round all numeric features to 2 decimal places before aggregation; reduces floating-point noise and can improve model stability.
actions.context_notes: Widely used AMEX-specific trick; rounding reduces precision noise from the competition's synthetic float data.

actions.reasoning: Standardize numeric features (zero mean, unit variance) for Keras/neural network model compatibility.

actions.reasoning: Clip extreme values at 1st and 99th percentile to reduce outlier influence.

actions.reasoning: Apply log1p transform to skewed financial features to reduce distributional skew.
